channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Carlton Vale/ Hansel Road, 20th July
item.description:
I reported a issue yesterday about heavy detritus in the channels with weeds. You have closed the job as fixed but the channel still remains the same. Please send someone back, I will be referring this to my local MP for being dishonest and saying the non has been done when it hasn't. This road is always dirty, why do I pay my council tax to live like this

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hansel Road, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead goose, 20th July
item.description:
Dead goose was left on the side, looks like it was hit then moved by the park
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Main Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Extreme Noise, 20th July
item.description:
Road work at 9:30PM at Urswick Road. Extremely high noise (all road workers using ear mufflers) right next to my house. I have a newborn baby who couldn’t sleep due to this noise. If this was done by a civilian at this hour it would anti social behaviour

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Halidon Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Roof in Poor Condition, 20th July
item.description:
Many roof tiles are missing. Making shelter unsafe to use due to potential more damage and tiles falling through into shelter. Barriers appeared abiut 1 week ago, but public keep moving barriers and using shelter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bourne Road, Bexley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping building rubbish outside 34-39 Allendale Close, 20th July
item.description:
Flytipping building rubbish outside 34-39 Allendale Close for removal please. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Allendale Close, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge or freezer dumped, 20th July
item.description:
Under counter fridge or freezer unsure which. Has been dumped on the edge of the railway bridge on the path and has been there for almost 2 weeks already.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southend Lane, Southend, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stop saying things are fixed when they are not, 20th July
item.description:
Weeds still there, says it was fixed but the pathway is still covered. Please remove all not just one or two

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hawthorne Crescent, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coal or bbq charcoal, 20th July
item.description:
Been here for over a month

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1F, Flodden Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

Graffiti

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Broadway Market, Hackney, London

item.title: Dumped black broken sofa, 20th July
item.description:
This has been here for at least 3 weeks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Flodden Road, Camberwell, Greater London

item.title: Bent sign to side of 37 Alexandra Road on Ravenstone Road, 20th July
item.description:
Sign is bent/leaning…

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Ravenstone Road, Hornsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Near 164 broadhurst garden, 20th July
item.description:
The pedestrian bridge over the tracks is completely overgrown and dangerous. Drug addicts are using it because it is overgrown and conceals their drug usage. Kids use the foot bridge making them vulnerable to an attack.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 164, Broadhurst Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping Daneville Road for removal, 20th July
item.description:
Flytipping Daneville Road for removal please. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Daneville Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins are open and attracting foces and vermin., 20th July
item.description:
The local bin men have explained that the bins are to stay on the road side by the park opposite silver house due to them not being able to access the bins at the end of the block where the enclosure is located. Vermin has been seen nearby.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Scoble Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping on Jelf Road, 20th July
item.description:
There is a pile of rubbish and furniture left on the corner of Jelf Road. Some of the rubbish has letters with an address on it just around the corner. Can the rubbish be cleared up? And also the perpetrators have some action taken against them?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Jelf Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: At the top of public steps, 20th July
item.description:
This bush hasnt been cut for 18 months at least. It has swarms of bees, wasps and flies every day that sting when walking past as the bush is so over grown u have to walk sideways. Its outside 138 southover
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Southover, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: It’s bent and in an awful condition, 20th July
item.description:
I reported this item before and you bounced back the problem saying I should contact post office engineering - but there’s not a lot I can do and it will remain unresolved and in a dreadful state as they don’t reply or even acknowledge receipt of my email. The post box is an Edward VII reign..only a few hundred remain nationally as not many were produced in the first place. I would like you to forward it to the conservation officer - I emailed them too and have received the same response as Royal Mail…nothing. I understand that as the post box in question is a heritage asset it is protected under a national Joint Heritage Agreement between Royal Mail and Historic England. I would therefore like to flag the state of disrepair to a conservation officer within the council so that you can launch a formal inquiry with Royal Mail designated heritage team under the rules of the national agreement to ensure that the asset is not to decay. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Alexandra Road, Harringay, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uncollected bins, 20th July
item.description:
Many residents have complained about this situation and they are repeatedly ignored. Bins repeatedly not being collected, attracting fly tipping, rats and creating a hazard by blocking the footpath and a foul smell for residents above. Please fix asap

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Westgate Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead tree, 20th July
item.description:
Please replace 🙏

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 50, King Henry's Road, Chalk Farm, Greater London

item.description:
